page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8i.4xlarge is an x86_64 architecture compute-optimized virtual machine instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8475B processor operating at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance does not include any bundled local storage, requiring external block storage volumes for operating system and application data. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps. The lack of integrated local storage and GPU accelerators represents a design tradeoff, focusing resources entirely on dedicated CPU execution and network throughput.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU allocation make this instance suitable for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. This hardware profile is optimized for workloads such as high-traffic web serving, batch processing, media encoding, and distributed analytics where CPU performance is the primary bottleneck.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8i.4xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8475B processor running at 2.7 GHz. The instance features 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. This resource configuration is designed for compute-intensive tasks where dedicated CPU allocation is critical. It is well-suited for workloads such as batch processing, web serving, and media encoding that require consistent CPU performance without the overhead of high memory capacity or local storage.
